text:
Market today: Chips are having their best quarter in years but headline #3 flags a real risk - valuation concentration in semis is at its highest since 2015. That's a direct warning for a book like yours, 90% tech/chips on margin. The Cleveland Fed chief also warned AI could reignite inflation and force rate hikes, which hits growth/tech names hardest.

Your holdings: SOXL (+5.69%) is a 3x leveraged chip ETF - with semis at a valuation extreme and rate-hike talk back, this is the most dangerous position you own on margin; watch it closely. PTNM (-15.25%) is your only red position, a tiny spec with a stop at $9.50 - it's close to triggering, no reason to hold through that level. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no trading history; margin amplifies any sudden drop.

Watch: Whether chip names crack on the valuation-risk headline. PTNM vs. its $9.50 stop.

Bottom line: Your book is crowded in exactly the sector flashing a rare concentration warning - protect your leveraged and speculative positions before adding anything new.
plays: SOXL: tighten your mental stop or trim a slice now - 3x leverage plus a rare semis valuation warning plus rate-hike talk is a bad combo on margin. Current stop $200, consider raising it closer to $230-240.
PTNM: honor the $9.50 stop, no exceptions - it's down 15%, it's tiny, it's speculative, and there's no scanner setup or catalyst to justify holding through that level.
No new buys today - semis are flagging a valuation extreme, rate risk is back on the table, and your book is already heavily concentrated. Hold what's working, protect what isn't.
